commands_run: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m money_model_architect.cli chat --business-dir /Users/benjaminmackenzie/Dev/money-model-architect/evals/runs/next_action/scenario_holdout/tooluse_v1_020/business_dir --message 'what does gross profit mean in plain English?'
final_answer: Gross profit is the money left after you subtract the direct cost of making or delivering what you sold. In plain English: if you sell something for $100 and it costs you $40 to provide it, your gross profit is $60. It does not include overhead like rent, admin salaries, software, or taxes.
